text: One of the most important skills in programming is learning how to debug effectively. The first step is identifying bugs in your code, which often involves noticing incorrect outputs or unexpected behavior. Instead of randomly changing code, experienced developers use a systematic approach: they reproduce the problem, isolate the possible cause, and test their fixes step by step. Helpful debugging tools include integrated development environments with breakpoints, which allow you to pause execution and inspect variable values. Additionally, logging—adding temporary print statements to track what your program is doing—can reveal hidden issues. A calm, patient attitude combined with these strategies makes debugging much less frustrating.
